sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check the new agent position after moving RIGHT
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']

# Find the blue agent (node 6 was the agent before, now it should have moved)
blue_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'b']
for n in blue_nodes:
    print(f"Blue node {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}, children={n['children']}, hash={n['hash']}")

# Check what the red path looks like now
red_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'R']
for n in red_nodes:
    print(f"Red node {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Let me also check the white passages
white_nodes = [n for n in nodes if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] < 100]
for n in white_nodes:
    print(f"White node {n['id']}: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Let me look at the ASCII for the maze area to understand the layout
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

# The maze is roughly rows 7-55, cols 13-50
# Let me print the maze area more compactly
for i in range(7, 56):
    line = ascii_lines[i]
    # Extract cols 13-50
    maze_row = line[13:51]
    # Replace W with . for readability, B with #, b with @, R with r
    readable = maze_row.replace('W', '.').replace('B', '#').replace('b', '@').replace('R', 'r').replace('w', '-')
    print(f"{i:2d}: {readable}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
